entries.title: University Admission Basics
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: Explain university admission basics
What is the admission process?
entries.content: University admissions usually evaluate academic marks, entrance exams, and document verification. Students should track registration dates, merit list publication, counseling, and final fee payment so they do not miss the confirmation window.

entries.title: Document Verification Window
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What happens in document verification?
Which documents are checked in admissions?
entries.content: Document verification checks identity proof, marksheets, photographs, signatures, category certificates, and application details. If a file is rejected, the student should re-upload the corrected version within the correction window instead of creating a duplicate application.

entries.title: Counseling Round Planning
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What is a counseling round?
How should I prepare for counseling rounds?
entries.content: A counseling round is the stage where eligible students review available seats, set preferences, and receive allotment results. Students should keep payment methods and required documents ready because the acceptance window after allotment is often short.

entries.title: Merit List Interpretation
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How do I read a merit list?
What does merit rank mean?
entries.content: A merit list usually shows the candidate rank, category, score, and allotment eligibility. Students should compare their rank with the cutoff history and keep backup program choices ready instead of depending on only one high-demand option.

entries.title: Seat Allotment Confirmation
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What happens after seat allotment?
How do I confirm my allotted seat?
entries.content: After seat allotment, the student must accept the seat, pay the required fee, and complete final reporting if instructed. Delayed payment or missing the acceptance step can automatically release the seat to the next candidate.

entries.title: Correction Window Guidance
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What is a correction window?
How do I fix mistakes in my application?
entries.content: The correction window allows a student to fix data entry errors such as name spelling, marks, uploaded documents, or category details. Students should use the official edit flow, save screenshots of the update, and recheck the submitted form after correction.

entries.title: Admission Deadline Tracking
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How do I track admission deadlines?
What deadlines matter during admissions?
entries.content: Students should track registration closing dates, exam dates, admit card release, counseling schedules, and fee payment deadlines in one checklist. A shared calendar with reminders helps avoid missing late-night or weekend deadlines.

entries.title: Profile Creation Checklist
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What should I check while creating an admission profile?
How do I register correctly?
entries.content: Profile creation should use the same name, date of birth, email, and mobile number that appear on official documents. Students should keep login credentials safe because later corrections, admit card access, and counseling updates depend on that account.

entries.title: Choice Filling Strategy
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How should I fill college choices?
What is the best choice filling strategy?
entries.content: Choice filling should balance dream options, realistic options, and safe options instead of listing only the most competitive programs. Preference order matters, so students should rank options according to genuine interest before locking the form.

entries.title: Entrance Exam Preparation Basics
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How should I prepare for entrance exams?
What is a simple entrance exam strategy?
entries.content: A strong entrance exam plan combines syllabus mapping, weekly revision, timed practice, and error review. Students should analyze weak topics after each mock test rather than only counting the number of hours studied.

entries.title: Admit Card Readiness
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What should I check on my admit card?
How do I prepare after downloading the admit card?
entries.content: Students should verify the exam date, reporting time, center address, photo visibility, and ID instructions on the admit card. It is safer to print multiple copies and visit the center area in advance if travel is unfamiliar.

entries.title: Fee Payment Safety
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How do I safely pay admission fees?
What should I do after fee payment?
entries.content: Admission fees should be paid only through the official portal or approved banking channel. Students should download the receipt immediately, verify the transaction status on the portal, and keep a copy for reporting day.

entries.title: Scholarship Application Timing
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: When should I apply for scholarships?
How do scholarship timelines fit with admission?
entries.content: Scholarship applications often open near admission or shortly after enrollment confirmation. Students should collect income proofs, category certificates, bank details, and institution letters early because these documents take time to arrange.

entries.title: Hostel Application Basics
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How does hostel application work?
When should I apply for hostel accommodation?
entries.content: Hostel applications usually open after or alongside admission confirmation and may require a separate form. Students should check room availability, fee schedule, security deposit, curfew rules, and documents such as admission proof and guardian ID.

entries.title: Transport and Commute Planning
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How should I plan my commute after admission?
What should I check for transport support?
entries.content: Commute planning should consider travel time, safety, reporting hours, and transport cost before finalizing enrollment. Students who travel daily should test the route once and keep a backup travel plan for exam days or bad weather.

entries.title: Admission Eligibility Check
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: Am I eligible for this admission?
What eligibility rules should I check before applying?
entries.content: Admission eligibility usually depends on the required academic marks, age limits, subject combinations, entrance score, and category rules in the official notice. Students should compare their profile with the published checklist before applying so they do not waste a seat on an ineligible program.

entries.title: Category Certificate Verification
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How is a category certificate verified?
What should I do if my category certificate is rejected?
entries.content: Category certificate verification checks whether the certificate is current, issued by the correct authority, and matches the applicant's record. Students should keep a scanned copy, the original certificate, and any supporting affidavit ready if the portal asks for correction.

entries.title: Cutoff and Seat Planning
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How do I use cutoff trends for admissions?
How should I plan seats around my cutoff?
entries.content: Cutoff planning means comparing your score or rank with past cutoff trends to estimate where you may get a seat. A practical strategy is to list dream, realistic, and safe options so the final choice order reflects both ambition and backup support.

entries.title: Reporting Day Checklist
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What should I bring on reporting day?
How do I prepare for final admission reporting?
entries.content: Reporting day usually requires the admission letter, fee receipt, ID proof, photographs, and original documents for final inspection. Students should arrive early, keep photocopies in order, and confirm the office hours because missed reporting can cancel the seat.

entries.title: Withdrawal and Refund Policy
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What is the refund policy if I withdraw admission?
How do admission withdrawal rules work?
entries.content: Withdrawal and refund rules explain how much fee can be returned if a student cancels admission within the official timeline. The safest habit is to read the refund table before paying any non-refundable amount and save the confirmation email or receipt.

entries.title: Gap Year Documentation
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: Do I need a gap year certificate?
What documents explain a study gap?
entries.content: If a student has taken a gap year, the institution may ask for a gap certificate, written explanation, or supporting documents such as work records or medical papers. The key is to tell the truth, keep the explanation short, and match the dates across all submitted forms.

entries.title: Migration and Transfer Certificate
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: When do I need a migration certificate?
How do I get a transfer certificate for college?
entries.content: A migration certificate or transfer certificate is often needed when moving from one institution or board to another. Students should request it early because the issuing office may take time, and the form may need previous college enrollment details.

entries.title: Hostel Allocation Priority
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How is hostel allotment decided?
Who gets hostel priority first?
entries.content: Hostel allocation usually depends on distance from campus, availability, gender-specific rules, and whether the student is a first-year or senior applicant. Students who need housing should apply as soon as the window opens and keep backup accommodation options ready.

entries.title: Entrance Scorecard Review
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: What should I check in my scorecard?
How do I review an entrance exam scorecard?
entries.content: An entrance scorecard should be checked for candidate name, roll number, subject-wise marks, total score, percentile, and rank. If anything is wrong, students should contact the exam helpdesk immediately and keep screenshots of the discrepancy.

entries.title: Counseling Mock Run
entries.author: Ankita Team
sample_queries: How do I practice for counseling rounds?
What is a counseling mock run?
entries.content: A counseling mock run is a rehearsal of the seat-selection and document-submission process before the real counseling round. Students can use it to test login access, understand the locking step, and decide the order of preferences before the actual deadline.
